response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience (TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, Java, C#), but the role demands 8 years of C++ as a hard minimum and deep large-scale distributed/cloud compute platform leadership. The resume shows only a brief early-career C/C++ stint at National Instruments (~6 months in 2000-2001) and no evidence of building or leading internal cloud compute platforms, distributed systems at scale, or formal tech-lead mentoring of cloud/DevOps engineers. Strong craftsmanship signal, but core technical requirements are not met.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with craftsmanship focus (TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based)
Demonstrated mentoring/teaching via workshops, dojos, and multi-module courses
Has shipped low-latency, high-throughput services and run platforms on Kubernetes
concerns: Only ~6 months of documented C/C++ work — far below the required 8 years
No clear experience leading an internal cloud compute platform or large-scale distributed infra
No explicit technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers in production cloud architecture

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform / large-scale distributed systems leadership
Strong mentoring, teaching, and software craftsmanship track record
